svg.title: 15 Vector Databases That Matter in Mid-2026
svg.desc: A curated comparison of 15 vector databases, their strengths, best uses, and documented production adopters, checked through July 10, 2026.
svg.text: 15 Vector Databases That Matter in Mid-2026
A curated guide — what they are, where they shine, and who runs them in production
Curation criteria: documented production adoption · momentum (GitHub traction, funding, revenue) · developer mindshare — checked July 10, 2026

g.text: Fully managed vector database with on-demand or
live freshness, metadata filtering, and hybrid
dense + sparse retrieval. Native BM25 full-text
search and BYOC are in public preview. A leading
Best for: Production RAG and semantic search
with zero ops overhead, scaling from prototype
Serverless vector + full-text search built from
first principles on object storage (S3 / GCS)
designs, warm queries stay sub-10ms p50. Runs
breakout growth story of the category.
Best for: Millions of per-user namespaces —
code, workspace and agent-memory search — at
built-in vectorizer modules that embed data at
ingest, BM25 + vector hybrid search, and standout
multi-tenancy — tens of thousands of isolated
tenants per cluster. HNSW supports compression;
disk-based HFresh became GA in June 2026.
Data) whose compute and storage scale
CAGRA — plus hybrid sparse / dense search at
huge scale. 3.0 beta adds zero-copy lake queries.
Best for: Massive-scale workloads: video dedup,
ads and recommendations, multimodal search on
Rust-built vector search engine known for raw
speed and memory efficiency. Fuses metadata
filtering directly into HNSW traversal, with
scalar, product, and binary quantization for up
for its minimal API — collection to query in a
few lines of Python. Runs embedded in your app
Chroma Cloud adds a Rust distributed core over
object storage. A popular prototyping choice in
Best for: RAG prototypes, agent memory, and
dev-speed-first AI apps that can grow into
Open-source embedded vector database on the
Lance columnar format — runs in-process or over
indexes free you from RAM limits; multimodal
tables and zero-copy versioning serve search,
analytics, and training alike. $30M Series A in
Best for: Local-first AI, edge deployments, and
in-process, open-source vector DB from Alibaba's
Proxima, the engine behind Taobao search and
Alipay face payment. Hybrid vector + full-text +
WAL durability and mobile support. Version 0.5
added DiskANN; 12K+ GitHub stars within months.
Best for: Local / edge RAG, on-device semantic
search, desktop AI apps. Young: no third-party
Open-source Postgres extension adding a vector
type with exact and ANN search (HNSW, IVFFlat).
Embeddings live next to relational data — you
keep joins, transactions, ACID, and SQL
filtering — supported by every major managed
adds DiskANN-style indexing on top, fueling a
Best for: Teams already on PostgreSQL adding
semantic search or RAG up to tens of millions of
The most widely deployed search engine, now a
full vector database: Lucene dense_vector fields
with HNSW kNN combine BM25, vectors, and the
ELSER sparse model in one query. Quantization
Best for: Enterprise hybrid lexical + semantic
search over large corpora, and existing Elastic
IVF via Faiss and Lucene, disk-based and 1-bit
become one of the most widely deployed vector
Best for: Enterprises on OpenSearch / ELK adding
Vector search beside operational JSON with no
separate store to sync. HNSW ANN, metadata
and dedicated Atlas Search Nodes. Since June
2026, Search and Vector Search also support
Best for: MongoDB shops adding RAG, semantic
search, or recommendations tightly coupled to
Memory-first vector search through Redis Search:
FLAT, HNSW, and SVS-VAMANA over HASH or JSON,
plus native Vector Sets. FT.HYBRID fuses vector
and full-text results; numeric, tag, and geo
filters stay co-located. Doubles as a semantic
Best for: Real-time AI — chatbots, agents, fraud
checks, live recommendations — where latency is
Open-source AI search and serving platform (born
at Yahoo) that runs vector, lexical BM25, and
structured search plus ML-model ranking with
ONNX inference in a single engine per query —
real-time mutable indexes at billions of
Best for: Web-scale RAG, e-commerce search, and
relational, time-series, and vector search in
one engine — repositioned in 2025–26 as an
the latest stable line. $23M Series A extension
Vector search remains a secondary capability.
Best for: Agent memory and apps wanting graph +
document + vector in one database instead of

svg.text: ANN libraries (FAISS, Turbovec, hnswlib, ScaNN, DiskANN…) are covered in the companion infographic. Also notable: pgvectorscale & ParadeDB (Postgres add-ons) · ClickHouse & Couchbase (vector as a feature).

The right choice depends on your use case — in production it's common to combine more than one. Logos are official marks of their respective owners (pgvector shown with the PostgreSQL mark — the project has no logo of its own).
Claims checked against case studies, engineering blogs, release notes & funding announcements through July 10, 2026; estimates are labeled.
